evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer is a thin facade delegating to three algorithm classes (SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) — responsibilities are clearly separated.
CollocateProfileComparator has a single coherent algorithm split into two named phases (buildRawCollocateProfiles, buildCollocateProfileList) with accurate Javadoc.
ExplorationService interface groups three related exploration modes with clear rationale documented in the Javadoc — intentional bundling, not a god interface.

viz/RelationEdgeType.java is a byte-for-byte duplicate of model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.java. The viz/package-info.java explicitly says the canonical home is model/exploration/, but the viz copy was never deleted.
viz/Edge.java (a record) still imports and uses viz/RelationEdgeType rather than the canonical model/exploration/RelationEdgeType, creating two parallel type hierarchies for the same concept.

No external callers import viz/Edge or viz/RelationEdgeType (confirmed via grep); the api layer and tests exclusively use model/exploration/Edge and model/exploration/RelationEdgeType — so viz/Edge.java and viz/RelationEdgeType.java are effectively dead code whose presence misleads developers about which type to use.
No god-function or excessive parameter list issues found in the seed files or their direct collaborators.

strengths: Responsibilities decomposed into focused algorithm classes (SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) rather than one monolithic explorer — each class has a single coherent job.
SemanticFieldExplorer acts as a documented, intentional facade; its Javadoc accurately describes why bundling the three modes under one interface is warranted.
ExplorationService interface groups methods that share corpus infrastructure without mixing in unrelated concerns.
CollocateProfileComparator.buildCollocateProfileList performs a well-reasoned two-pass numeric computation with inline rationale for why two passes are unavoidable.
design_coherence.issue_character: The single structural issue is an incomplete migration: a canonical type (RelationEdgeType and its companion Edge) was moved to model/exploration/ but the old viz/ copies were never deleted, leaving two definitions of the same concept in the codebase.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The exploration module is well-decomposed — focused classes, earned abstractions, no parameter sprawl. However, the duplicate viz/RelationEdgeType and viz/Edge types create a genuine design coherence problem: a developer adding a new edge type must know which package is canonical (and currently the viz copy points to the wrong one). The package-info even documents the intended canonical location while the old copy persists unused. This sits squarely in the 80-range 'solid but uneven' band where a developer would hit a moment of 'which type do I use?' friction. The otherwise strong decomposition keeps the score from dropping further.

issues.summary: viz/RelationEdgeType and viz/Edge are dead duplicates of their model/exploration/ counterparts, creating two competing type definitions for the same concept.
evidence: viz/RelationEdgeType.java is an exact copy of model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.java — same constants, same label() method, same toString() override.
viz/Edge.java imports viz/RelationEdgeType (the old copy) rather than model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.
viz/package-info.java explicitly states that Edge and RelationEdgeType 'live in model/exploration/' to avoid an api→viz dependency, but the viz/ copies were never deleted.
No Java file outside viz/ imports viz/RelationEdgeType or viz/Edge — both are unused by the api layer and all tests, which exclusively use the model/exploration/ versions.
issues.suggestion: Delete viz/RelationEdgeType.java and update viz/Edge.java to import model/exploration/RelationEdgeType instead of the viz-local copy. If viz/Edge.java itself is also unused outside the viz package (no external callers found), delete it too and rely solely on model/exploration/Edge.

root_causes: A type migration moved RelationEdgeType (and Edge) from viz/ to model/exploration/ to break an api→viz import dependency, but the cleanup step (deleting the old viz/ copies and updating viz/Edge.java's import) was not completed.
possible_false_positives: The boilerplate_duplication signal on CollocateProfileComparator vs SemanticFieldExplorer imports (lines 7/6) — these are normal Java import blocks, not a design coherence issue worth acting on.

The boilerplate_duplication signal on ExplorationService vs SemanticFieldExplorer (lines 10/16) — similar observation: shared import groups reflecting shared domain dependencies, not structural duplication.
